metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before the production flip, verify empirically (on a test change or from Gerrit 3.14.1 source/docs) what patchset changekind Gerrit's server-side Rebase-If-Necessary submit assigns — then add the determined outcome to AC2's documentation and confirm the copyCondition values are correct for that behavior.
findings.finding: AC2 depends on knowing whether Gerrit 3.14.1's server-side Rebase-If-Necessary submit creates a new patchset (and if so, what changekind it assigns) to determine whether the LLM-Review TRIVIAL_REBASE carry still applies correctly. ADR 0041 (line 3) explicitly flags this as 'not re-derived here and needs human review.' The plan defers the answer to the executor ('document any copyCondition change') without a pre-stated outcome, leaving a capability gap: if RIN's server-side rebase does NOT produce a TRIVIAL_REBASE patchset (or produces no patchset at all), the LLM-Review copyCondition carry — a key security and correctness assumption — behaves differently than the current FFO model, and the existing copyCondition values may need adjustment.
findings.location: AC2 — Revisit Verified copyCondition; also docs/adr/0041-llm-review-carry-trivial-rebase.md line 3 caveat

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC2's open-ended 'document any copyCondition change' with a decided outcome: either (a) state 'copyCondition is unchanged and the existing Vote-Copied behavior is sufficient under Rebase-If-Necessary because …' or (b) state the specific copyCondition value to set and why, so the executor has no design decision to make.
findings.finding: AC2 defers the real design decision about copyCondition to the executor ('document any copyCondition change') without a stated default or predetermined outcome, making it a placeholder 'check if' bullet rather than a decided specification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Revisit Verified copyCondition'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC2 as: 'Verified copyCondition: confirm that copyCondition = COPIES_ON_TRIVIAL_REBASE (or equivalent) is already set and sufficient — no change required — because Gerrit Rebase-If-Necessary performs a server-side rebase that preserves the vote under this condition; proving command: grep copyCondition infra/gerrit/project.config and assert the value matches <EXPECTED_VALUE>.' If a change IS required, specify the target value in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2: State the predetermined conclusion in the plan — under Rebase-If-Necessary, Gerrit's atomic submit-time server-side rebase creates no new patchset in the change, so `copyCondition` is never evaluated at submit; both existing copyConditions (LLM-Review: NO_CODE_CHANGE OR MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE OR TRIVIAL_REBASE; Verified: NO_CODE_CHANGE) are correct and unchanged. The AC should instruct the executor to verify this claim and close the ADR 0041 'needs human review' flag, not to rediscover it.
findings.finding: AC2 defers the LLM-Review TRIVIAL_REBASE copyCondition decision to the executor as a discovery task ('confirm... document any copyCondition change') without stating the expected outcome. ADR 0041 explicitly flags that the TRIVIAL_REBASE + Rebase-If-Necessary interaction 'needs human review.' The correct answer is mechanically determinable: under RIN, Gerrit's submit-time server-side rebase is atomic and creates NO new patchset in the change, so copyCondition is never evaluated at submit time — the existing TRIVIAL_REBASE carry on LLM-Review is inert at submit and harmless for client-side pre-submit rebases. Leaving the outcome unspecified risks an executor mistakenly removing TRIVIAL_REBASE from LLM-Review's copyCondition, which would break the pre-submit client-side rebase carry that still occurs.
findings.location: AC2: 'Revisit Verified copyCondition: under Rebase-If-Necessary Gerrit integrates at submit (no review re-patchset), so confirm the submit requirement still gates on Verified+1 & LLM-Review+1 correctly; document any copyCondition change.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC2's open 'document any copyCondition change' with: 'Confirm that copyCondition is unchanged: RIN's server-side rebase is atomic (no intermediate patchset), so TRIVIAL_REBASE is never evaluated at submit; the existing LLM-Review and Verified copyConditions are correct as-is. Update the ADR 0041 and ADR 0025 header notes to close the 'needs human review' flag with this conclusion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2: Specify the predetermined copyCondition outcome — state explicitly whether TRIVIAL_REBASE should remain on LLM-Review's copyCondition (and why) given that RIN server-side submit does not mint a new patchset, so the executor has a concrete assertion to verify rather than an open-ended research task.
findings.finding: AC2 defers the copyCondition processing-protocol decision to the executor ('document any copyCondition change') without stating the correct outcome. Under Rebase-If-Necessary, Gerrit rebases server-side at submit WITHOUT minting a new patchset object, so the TRIVIAL_REBASE changekind (present in LLM-Review's copyCondition) is never triggered by a RIN server-side submit-rebase. The plan references this protocol but leaves its disposition undefined, creating an executor-facing ambiguity about whether copyCondition changes are needed.
findings.location: AC2: 'Revisit Verified copyCondition: under Rebase-If-Necessary Gerrit integrates at submit (no review re-patchset), so confirm the submit requirement still gates on Verified+1 & LLM-Review+1 correctly; document any copyCondition change.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Expected outcome: no copyCondition change is needed — RIN's server-side submit rebase does not create a new Gerrit patchset object, so changekind tokens are never evaluated on the submit path; the existing copyConditions remain correct for client-side re-uploads. Verify by confirming Gerrit 3.14.1 RIN submit does not appear as a new patchset in the change history.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the Gerrit 3.14.1 documentation (or release notes) that explicitly describes whether Rebase-If-Necessary creates a new patchset object before submitting, and confirm how this interacts with the Verified and LLM-Review copyCondition configurations; document the verified behavior in AC2's 'copyCondition change' note.
findings.finding: The plan does not cite or verify the exact Gerrit 3.14.1 patchset-lifecycle behavior of a server-side Rebase-If-Necessary submit: specifically, whether RIN creates a new patchset object (triggering copyCondition re-evaluation and potentially dropping Verified/LLM-Review votes before submit) or atomically rebases-and-submits without minting a new patchset. AC2 acknowledges uncertainty ('confirm the submit requirement still gates… correctly; document any copyCondition change') but leaves the underlying mechanism unresolved. Under FFO the copyCondition semantics were clear (a new patchset always preceded submit); under RIN the server-side rebase path may behave differently, which is load-bearing for the gate's GerriScary posture.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Revisit Verified copyCondition… confirm the submit requirement still gates on Verified+1 & LLM-Review+1 correctly'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Per [Gerrit config-project-config §submit-type](https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/config-project-config.html#submit-type), RIN submits the rebased commit directly without creating a Gerrit patchset object, so copyCondition is NOT re-evaluated at submit time — the votes already present at submit-click govern. Verified behavior confirmed against 3.14.1 by DRY_RUN + operator observation.' If the verification reveals RIN does mint a patchset, document that Verified will need a broader copyCondition (e.g., add TRIVIAL_REBASE) to avoid breaking the landing flow.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document handling of open changes with existing Autosubmit votes (e.g., confirm there are none, or specify how to clear them before the label is removed).
findings.finding: The plan does not address what happens to open/in-flight changes that already carry an Autosubmit=+1 vote at the time the label is removed. Gerrit's behavior with votes referencing a now-missing label is unspecified, which could cause those changes to become unsubmittable or display error states.
findings.location: AC line 1 (label removal) and AC line 4 (apply in quiet window)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-cutover check: query for any open changes with Autosubmit votes and either remove those votes or wait/communicate with their authors before proceeding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability step: specify which Gerrit logs/metrics/alerts to monitor during and immediately after the config apply (e.g., submit error rate, replication errors, any bot error logs).
findings.finding: The plan has no observability specification for the production cutover: no mention of which Gerrit logs, metrics, or alerts operators should watch during and after the config apply to detect unexpected behavior (e.g., submit failures, error-rate spikes, unexpected rebase collisions).
findings.location: AC line 4: 'apply refs/meta/config via the admin key … in a quiet window'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether any non-bot Contributors rely on the `rebase` grant (e.g., REST rebase endpoint usage); if so, update the plan's impact/rollback scope to include restoring `rebase = group Contributors` separately from the submit-type revert.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the rebase ACL to be removed is 'the per-ref rebase (rebase-on-behalf) grant that only the bot needed,' but the actual grant in project.config (lines 75–76) covers the entire `Contributors` group AND `Administrators` — not just the bot. Other Contributors also hold this right. The claim that 'only the bot needed' it is an unbacked characterization; if any Contributor currently uses the rebase REST endpoint for their own changes, removing this grant is a behavior change for them (not just the bot), and the plan's rollback/impact scope is understated.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'remove … the per-ref rebase (rebase-on-behalf) grant that only the bot needed'; project.config lines 63–76
findings.suggested_fix: In AC line 1, qualify the assertion: 'the per-ref rebase grant (currently: `rebase = group Contributors` + `rebase = group Administrators`) — confirm no non-bot Contributor currently uses the REST rebase endpoint before removing.' In the Back-out line, add restoring the rebase grant as a second step alongside the submit-type revert.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the git commit message string in setup-project.sh line 148 to remove the references to the Autosubmit label and rebase/rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant that this plan retires.
findings.finding: setup-project.sh's git commit message (line 148) still explicitly mentions 'Autosubmit label (auto-lander opt-in, epic f1fa)' and 'rebase/rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant (auto-lander FFO rebase, S5b)' — both items the plan removes. The plan's AC says 'Update infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh / smoke-check if they reference the removed label/ACL,' but the probe confirms the commit message string IS such a reference. The AC does not specifically call out updating this string, leaving it stale after the cutover.
findings.location: AC line 3: 'Update infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh / smoke-check if they reference the removed label/ACL'; setup-project.sh line 148
findings.suggested_fix: Change the commit message to omit the Autosubmit and rebase-grant clauses, e.g.: `"rebar project.config: LLM-Review + Verified labels + submit requirements + submit-type RIN + feature-branch ACLs + Contributors submit gate"`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` to AC-3's update scope: remove or replace §1b (lines 38–58) that assert Autosubmit label invariants, since those assertions are meaningless once the label is retired.
findings.finding: infra/scripts/config-check.sh has an entire named section (§1b, lines 38–58) that actively asserts Autosubmit label security invariants; it is an unenumerated consumer that should be cleaned up when the label is removed. The plan's AC-3 scope covers only infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh and smoke-check.sh, leaving this file unaddressed.
findings.location: AC-3 / infra/scripts/config-check.sh lines 38–58
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC-3 to read: 'Update infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh, smoke-check.sh, AND infra/scripts/config-check.sh (remove §1b Autosubmit invariant block) if they reference the removed label/ACL.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the setup-project.sh git commit message (line 148) to drop references to the retired Autosubmit label and rebase/rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant.
findings.finding: The setup-project.sh git commit message (line 148) explicitly names the Autosubmit label and rebase/rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant as part of the committed config; after removal this message will describe artifacts that no longer exist in the pushed project.config, creating a misleading audit trail in refs/meta/config history.
findings.location: AC-3 / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 148
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the commit message with one that reflects the current state, e.g.: 'rebar project.config: LLM-Review + Verified labels + submit requirements + Rebase-If-Necessary submit type + feature-branch ACLs + Contributors submit gate'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC5) to verify and document the feature-branch merge-back behavior under Rebase-If-Necessary: confirm that Gerrit refuses (rather than rebase-corrupts) a non-FF merge commit, that the MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE copyCondition carry still works correctly, and close the open 'needs human review' flags in ADR 0025 and ADR 0041.
findings.finding: The plan has no acceptance criterion covering how the feature-branch merge-back flow (the --no-ff merge commit pushed by feature-branch-drivers) behaves under Rebase-If-Necessary. Under RIN, Gerrit rebases regular changes but does NOT rebase merge commits — for a merge commit that does not fast-forward, Gerrit refuses the submit (same signal as a textual conflict). Both ADR 0025 and ADR 0041 carry explicit notes that 'how Rebase-If-Necessary interacts with the merge-back re-merge/re-CI cost is not re-derived here and needs human review.' The plan does not address this and has no test step for the feature-branch submit path.
findings.location: AC5: 'Verify a normal change lands via a plain Gerrit Submit... and a textual-conflict change is refused' — covers only the ordinary change path, not the feature-branch merge-back path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-production validation step: apply the submit-type change to a dev/staging Gerrit project (or a test project on the same server) and run AC5's normal-change + conflict-change verification there before the production quiet-window apply.
findings.finding: The plan applies the Gerrit submit-type flip directly to production with no mention of a lower environment (dev or staging Gerrit instance) where the Rebase-If-Necessary behavior — and its interaction with existing copyCondition configs for LLM-Review and Verified — can be validated before the production apply.
findings.location: AC4 (operator-attested apply) and AC5 (verification)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the git commit message in setup-project.sh (line 148) to remove references to 'Autosubmit label (auto-lander opt-in, epic f1fa)' and 'rebase/rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant (auto-lander FFO rebase, S5b)' and add a note about the Rebase-If-Necessary submit type.
findings.finding: AC3 says to update setup-project.sh where it references the removed label/ACL, but the plan does not call out that the git commit message on line 148 of setup-project.sh explicitly names 'Autosubmit label (auto-lander opt-in, epic f1fa) + rebase/rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant (auto-lander FFO rebase, S5b)' — both of which are being removed. Leaving a stale commit message means every future idempotent run of setup-project.sh will push a config with an inaccurate change description, causing operational confusion.
findings.location: AC3 ('Update infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh / smoke-check if they reference the removed label/ACL'); infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 148
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 148 to: git commit -q -m "rebar project.config: LLM-Review + Verified labels + submit requirements + submit-type Rebase-If-Necessary (was FFO, see ADR-0040 cutover) + feature-branch ACLs + Contributors submit gate"

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before editing project.config, confirm the exact git-config token for Rebase-If-Necessary in Gerrit 3.14.1 by checking the upstream docs (config-project-config.html §submit.action) or reading a live Gerrit instance's project.config where RIN is active — record the verified string next to the planned change.
findings.finding: The exact git-config value string for Rebase-If-Necessary in Gerrit 3.14.1's project.config is asserted as 'action = rebase if necessary' but is not verified against the Gerrit documentation or a live config read. The analogous 'action = fast forward only' is known-working (live and commented in project.config), making the convention plausible, but a string mismatch (e.g. 'rebase_if_necessary', 'rebaseIfNecessary', or 'REBASE_IF_NECESSARY') would silently fall back to the site default submit type rather than erroring, leaving the flip undetected until the smoke test.
findings.location: AC1 — infra/gerrit/project.config [submit] action = rebase if necessary
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan (AC1), replace the bare assertion with a cited source: e.g. 'set action = rebase if necessary (confirmed from Gerrit 3.14.1 docs: config-project-config.html §submit.action — value is lowercase with spaces, matching the fast forward only convention already live)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC3: Extend the scope to include infra/scripts/config-check.sh — either remove or update the Autosubmit security-invariant check (lines 38-58) so it does not emit 'skip: Autosubmit label not present yet (pre-S1 cutover)' in the permanent post-retirement state.
findings.finding: After the Autosubmit label is removed from project.config, the config-check.sh check 1b (the Autosubmit security-invariant guard, lines 43-58) will silently skip with the misleading message 'skip: Autosubmit label not present yet (pre-S1 cutover)' — a message that describes the pre-S1 state, not the post-retirement state. The plan's AC3 addresses setup-project.sh and smoke-check but does not mention updating or retiring this guard in config-check.sh.
findings.location: AC3: 'Update infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh / smoke-check if they reference the removed label/ACL.' / infra/scripts/config-check.sh lines 38-58
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the else branch message (line 57) with 'skip: Autosubmit label retired (post-ADR-0047 cutover — label removed)' or delete the entire check 1b block, and note this in AC3 alongside setup-project.sh and smoke-check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC2, e.g.: `grep -A5 'submit-requirement' infra/gerrit/project.config` to assert Verified+1 and LLM-Review+1 remain the only submit requirements, plus a Gerrit REST API call (`GET /projects/*/config` or `GET /changes/*/submit_requirements`) against a test change with exactly those two votes to confirm it is submittable.
findings.finding: AC2 provides no concrete proving command for 'confirm the submit requirement still gates on Verified+1 & LLM-Review+1 correctly'; 'confirm' is a hedge with no observable test or command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'confirm the submit requirement still gates on Verified+1 & LLM-Review+1 correctly'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC#6 back-out to explicitly cover restoration of the Autosubmit label definition, label-Autosubmit ACL, and rebase-on-behalf grant (not just the submit-type revert), e.g. by keeping the full pre-cutover project.config stanza in a versioned backup or git tag on refs/meta/config.
findings.finding: The back-out plan covers only the submit-type revert ('one-line submit-type revert to fast forward only') but does not address restoring the Autosubmit label definition, label-Autosubmit ACL, and per-ref rebase grant that are also being removed. A rollback that only restores FFO submit type but leaves the Autosubmit label absent would leave the bot non-functional if the rollback reason was bot-related.
findings.location: AC#6 (Back-out) + AC#1 (label/ACL removals)
findings.suggested_fix: Tag the current refs/meta/config as `pre-cutover-backup` before applying changes; document that back-out = `git push origin pre-cutover-backup:refs/meta/config` (restores all three: submit type, label, ACL simultaneously) rather than a line-level edit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-cutover step to AC#4 (or a new AC) that drains or explicitly cancels in-flight Autosubmit-bot-queued changes and notifies their authors, so no change is silently stranded at cutover.
findings.finding: The plan retires the Autosubmit label and the bot's rebase-on-behalf ACL grant without acknowledging what happens to changes currently in flight that carry an Autosubmit vote or are queued for bot submission. These changes would silently lose their submit path with no migration signal to their authors.
findings.location: AC#1 (label/ACL removal) and AC#4 (live apply in quiet window)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step to decommission or reconfigure the Autosubmit bot (or confirm it is already retired/no-ops) as part of this cutover, acknowledging the cross-component dependency being severed.
findings.finding: The plan retires the Autosubmit label and its ACL but never addresses what happens to the Autosubmit bot (or any automation) that votes on that label. If the bot still runs post-cutover and attempts to cast an Autosubmit+1 vote, Gerrit will silently discard or error on an unknown label — a hidden cross-component coupling with no acknowledgment or mitigation.
findings.location: AC1 / title ('retire Autosubmit label + rebase ACL')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or pre-flight step: 'Confirm the Autosubmit bot is decommissioned / disabled / reconfigured to no longer vote the Autosubmit label before or concurrent with removing the label from refs/meta/config.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step to (a) search for and update any contributor-facing docs (CONTRIBUTING.md, onboarding guides, wiki pages) that reference the Autosubmit label or FFO submit type, and (b) capture the FFO→RIN decision in an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a meaningful architectural change to the project's merge workflow (FFO + bot-driven Autosubmit → Rebase-If-Necessary with plain Submit) but does not capture this decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or any design document. Contributor-facing documentation (e.g., CONTRIBUTING.md, onboarding guides) that describe the Autosubmit flow or 'how to land a change' are not mentioned.
findings.location: Overall plan / AC1–AC6 (no doc/ADR step present)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Update CONTRIBUTING.md / docs to remove Autosubmit references and describe plain Submit flow. Add a one-paragraph ADR entry (or CLAUDE.md note) recording the rationale for retiring FFO+Autosubmit in favour of Rebase-If-Necessary.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all consumers of the Autosubmit label (bots, CI scripts, dashboards, submit-requirements) and specify their retirement or update order relative to the label removal step.
findings.finding: The plan removes the Autosubmit label and its ACL but does not enumerate or address the consumer(s) of that label — specifically the bot/automation that sets Autosubmit votes — leaving the retirement path incomplete. If the bot runs after the label is removed, it will receive an error (or silently misbehave) when attempting to post an Autosubmit vote on any open change.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'remove the Autosubmit label definition + label-Autosubmit ACL + the per-ref rebase (rebase-on-behalf) grant that only the bot needed'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step before AC1: 'Disable/update the Autosubmit bot so it no longer posts Autosubmit votes; confirm no open changes are awaiting Autosubmit=+1 before removing the label definition.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and document the bot/pipeline that was posting Autosubmit votes, and add an explicit step to decommission or reconfigure it before or alongside the label removal.
findings.finding: The plan removes the Autosubmit label definition and rebase-on-behalf ACL but does not acknowledge or address the actor (bot, pipeline, or script) that was posting Autosubmit+1 votes under the old regime. If that actor is still active after the cutover, it will hit errors posting a vote on a label that no longer exists, potentially breaking CI or causing alert noise.
findings.location: AC1: 'remove the Autosubmit label definition + label-Autosubmit ACL + the per-ref rebase (rebase-on-behalf) grant that only the bot needed'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC (or a sub-step to AC1) that reads: 'Confirm the Autosubmit-voting bot is disabled / reconfigured to no longer post Autosubmit votes; verify no active job references the Autosubmit label after cutover.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the back-out section to assert the FULL post-rollback schema state: specify that rollback must also re-add the Autosubmit label definition and ACL grants to project.config (not only revert the submit type), and document whether NoteDb vote records for the Autosubmit label on open changes are recoverable or permanently orphaned after removal (attach a `DATA LOSS RISK` note if not recoverable).
findings.finding: The rollback path states only 'one-line submit-type revert to fast forward only' but does not assert the full schema state after rollback. Removing the Autosubmit label definition deletes it from project.config (persisted in refs/meta/config), and Gerrit NoteDb may retain orphaned vote records for the removed label on any open changes. The described rollback only restores the submit type — it does not re-add the label definition or ACL grants — leaving the post-rollback config in an incorrect intermediate state where the submit type is reverted but the label/ACL are still absent.
findings.location: AC line 6 — 'Back-out: one-line submit-type revert to fast forward only'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a rollback checklist with two steps: (1) revert `[submit] action` to `fast forward only`; (2) restore the Autosubmit label block and label-Autosubmit ACL entries in project.config. Additionally, note that any open-change NoteDb votes for Autosubmit cast before the cutover are NOT automatically restored by re-adding the label definition — operators must either accept vote loss or manually re-apply votes for affected changes.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
